title: Crispy Sweet Ethiopian Almond Flatbread
description: A light, flaky flatbread sweetened with erythritol and spiced with whole black peppercorns, served with crisp pickled cucumber for a tangy contrast.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Grease a 9x9-inch baking pan or line with parchment.
In a large bowl, whisk together almond flour, ground flaxseed meal, erythritol, baking powder, sea salt, and whole black peppercorns until evenly mixed.
Add melted coconut oil, unsweetened almond milk, and lime juice to the dry mixture. Stir until a soft dough forms; it will be slightly sticky.
Transfer dough onto the prepared pan, using wet hands or a spatula to flatten and spread to an even thickness (about 1/4-inch).
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and let the flatbread cool for 5 minutes. Slice into 8 wedges.
Serve warm, garnished with pickled cucumber slices. Keep the remainder refrigerated; it stays crisp for up to 4 days.
